text:
Market today: Chip stocks started Q3 weak after a monster Q2 run - headline 3 says it plainly. That's the real driver for your book today. Meta's 9% pop on cloud/AI news lifted sentiment broadly, but semis are lagging, and that's where most of your risk lives.

Your holdings: SOXL (-14.52%) is the loudest alarm - it's a 3X leveraged ETF, decay is real, and you're sitting on a big loss with your stop at $200 (price $217). On margin, a gap down could slice through that fast. SPAL (-13.72%) is a brand-new, very volatile leveraged IPO - small cushion above your $21.38 stop at $21.70, one bad tick ends it. SMH (-2.5%) is quieter but confirms the sector headwind; watch it closely given it underpins your whole thesis.

Watch: Whether SMH can hold above $600 - a break there drags SOXL toward your stop hard. Also watch if Meta's AI cloud move pulls chip demand narrative positive.

Bottom line: Your leveraged positions are where margin risk is most acute right now - respect those stops.
plays: SOXL: Stop at $200 is your line - consider tightening it closer to current price given the -14.5% loss and sector weakness today. No reason to give back more on a 3X name.
SPAL: At $21.70 with a stop at $21.38, there's almost no room - either you're convicted or this one gets cut now. Margin makes a gap-through ugly.
No new buys today - sector is starting Q3 soft, your leveraged positions need attention first, and no scanner setup has a live catalyst strong enough to justify adding exposure here.
